message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "anime tactical simulator how to get units: Complete Guide 2026",
  description: "Master anime tactical simulator how to get units with our 2026 guide. Learn summoning, AFK farming for Igris, luck team builds, and evolution secrets.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Anime+Tactical+Unit+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Tactical Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **anime tactical simulator how to get units**: Primary methods include summoning with Gems, completing World quests, and farming specific dungeons for drops.
- **Luck Team Importance**: Building a dedicated Luck team with the **Fortune** trait can double your chances of pulling Mythic and Secret units.
- **Igris Acquisition**: Obtain the powerful Igris unit by completing the 120-kill quest in the **Awakening Dungeon** or relying on a 0.5% drop rate.
- **Tower Mode**: Prioritize clearing Tower floors to unlock **extra unit slots**, which is the fastest way to increase your team's total DPS.
- **Evolution Mechanics**: Save duplicate units and sell lower-tier ones for **Souls** to evolve your core Mythic squad.
</Callout>

In the competitive landscape of Roblox tower defense, understanding **anime tactical simulator how to get units** is the fundamental pillar of your success. Whether you are a newcomer starting on Planet Namek or a veteran pushing through World 3, your roster's strength dictates how efficiently you can clear waves and tackle high-level raids. This comprehensive guide covers every acquisition method available in 2026, from basic summoning mechanics to advanced AFK farming strategies for elite units like Igris. We will explore how to optimize your resource spending, leverage active codes for free pulls, and build a specialized luck-based setup to ensure your squad remains top-tier. By following this roadmap, you will learn exactly **anime tactical simulator how to get units** that can carry you through the toughest endgame content.

<Section icon={Map} title="Summoning Basics and Free Resource Management">

Summoning is the primary way most players interact with the **anime tactical simulator how to get units** system. By spending Gems at the central banners, you can pull for units ranging from Common to Secret rarities. In the early game, your priority should be securing a full team of Legendaries, which will serve as your baseline for World 1 progression.

**Video Highlights:**
- **Beginner Essentials**: Complete the tutorial immediately to unlock core summoning features.
- **Gamepass Strategy**: Prioritize the **+2 Units Equip** pass to instantly boost your field power.
- **Free-to-Play Tips**: Gems are easily farmable through quests and achievements, making most gamepasses accessible for F2P players.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="4-dVEfNcPkY" title="Anime Tactical Ultimate Beginners Guide + Codes" />

To maximize your pulls, you must utilize the active codes provided by the developers. These codes often grant thousands of Gems and Dungeon Keys, which are essential for early-game momentum.

| Code | Rewards | Requirement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **RELEASE** | 500 Gems, 5 Dungeon Keys | None |
| **UPDATE1** | 1000 Gems, 10 Potions | None |
| **DISCORD** | 3 Talent Rerolls, 2 Premium Rerolls | Join Roblox Group |
| **THANKYOU** | 5 Dungeon Keys, 500 Yen | None |
| **LAUNCH** | 1000 Gems, 10 Dungeon Keys | None |

<Callout type="tip" title="Pro Tip for Summoning">
Always check the current banner's pity counter. If you are close to a guaranteed Mythic, focus all your Gems on that specific banner rather than spreading them across different worlds.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Farming Elite Units: The Igris Method">

When discussing **anime tactical simulator how to get units**, the legendary unit Igris (Crimson Knight) is often the most sought-after. Unlike standard banner units, Igris requires specific dedication to the **Awakening Dungeon**. There are two main paths to obtaining this powerhouse: the RNG drop or the guaranteed quest.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Access the Awakening Dungeon">
    Travel to the Dungeon Town and locate the Awakening Dungeons portal. Ensure your team is at least level 50 before attempting this solo.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Accept the Crimson Knight Quest">
    Talk to the quest NPC to track your progress. You need **120 kills** on the Crimson Knight for a guaranteed Igris unit reward.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Optimize Your Clear Speed">
    Use your highest DPS units. Igris has significant HP, so traits like **Dead Eye** or **Annihilator** are highly recommended to reduce clear times.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Set Up AFK Farming">
    Many players use tools like TinyTask to automate the dungeon entry and combat loop. Ensure your camera is positioned correctly to auto-click the "Retry" or "Travel" buttons.
  </Step>
</Steps>

| Method | Chance/Requirement | Efficiency |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Direct Drop** | 0.5% per clear | Low (RNG dependent) |
| **Quest Completion** | 120 Total Kills | High (Guaranteed) |
| **AFK Farming** | ~20 clears per hour | Best for long-term gains |

<Callout type="warning" title="AFK Safety">
When AFK farming, ensure your character is positioned slightly to the left of the center in the Double Dungeon to avoid getting stuck on environmental assets when the boss spawns.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Building a Luck Team for Better Pulls">

A common mistake players make when figuring out **anime tactical simulator how to get units** is ignoring the Luck stat. Your base luck is usually around 1.0 to 4.0, but with a dedicated Luck Team, you can push this above 8.0, drastically increasing the frequency of Mythic pulls.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="The Fortune Trait">
    - **Effect**: +50% Luck per unit
    - **Scaling**: Stacks across your entire equipped roster
    - **Best for**: Mythic units with two trait slots
  </Card>
  <Card title="Luck Gear">
    - **Item**: Richie Armor
    - **Effect**: +20% Luck boost
    - **Source**: Rare drop from Raids
  </Card>
  <Card title="Luck Gamepasses">
    - **Tiers**: Lucky, Super, Ultra
    - **Benefit**: Permanent flat additions to your Luck stat
    - **Priority**: Buy after Unit Slots
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

To build this team, you should roll for the **Fortune** trait on units you don't intend to use for combat. Since Mythic units can hold two traits, they are the ideal candidates for "Double Fortune" rolls, which provide a massive 100% luck increase per slot.

<Callout type="success" title="Luck Comparison">
A standard team might have a luck value of **4.15**, while a fully optimized Luck Team can reach **8.79** or higher. This effectively doubles your chances of seeing a Secret unit on any given banner.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Unit Management: Keep, Fuse, or Sell?">

Knowing **anime tactical simulator how to get units** is only half the battle; you also need to manage your inventory efficiently. Not every unit is worth keeping, and some are more valuable as "food" for your stronger characters.

| Rarity | Action | Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Secret** | **KEEP** | Extremely rare, best endgame stats |
| **Mythic** | **KEEP/EVOLVE** | Primary DPS source, keep duplicates for evolution |
| **Legendary** | **KEEP/FUSE** | Early game core, use as XP fodder later |
| **Epic/Rare** | **FUSE** | Best used to level up your Mythic units |
| **Common** | **SELL** | Sell for **Souls** required in the evolution process |

Evolution is the key to late-game power. To evolve a unit, you typically need:
1. Multiple **Duplicate Copies** of the target unit.
2. **Souls**, which are obtained by selling units of the same rarity.
3. **Evolution Stones**, which drop from high-level raids and dungeons.

<Callout type="info" title="Evolution Tip">
Do not rush evolution in World 1 or 2. It is more resource-efficient to build a diverse team of level 50+ Mythics before spending rare Souls on a single evolved unit.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Maximizing Squad Size via Tower Mode">

The most direct way to improve your "how to get units" strategy is to increase the number of units you can actually use. **Tower Mode** is the only way to permanently unlock additional deployment slots without spending Robux.

<Checklist
  id="tower-progression"
  title="Critical Tower Milestones:"
  items={[
    "Clear Floor 10: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot",
    "Clear Floor 25: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot",
    "Clear Floor 50: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot and Rare Items",
    "Clear Floor 100: Unlocks +1 Unit Slot and a Secret Unit"
  ]}
/>

Each extra slot represents a massive jump in your total damage output. For example, moving from 4 to 5 units is a 25% increase in your team's theoretical DPS. Furthermore, the achievements linked to Tower Mode provide **Movement Medals**, which allow you to upgrade your attack range, making it easier to "insta-kill" mobs as you walk past them in the overworld.

<Callout type="warning" title="Tower Difficulty">
The difficulty in Tower Mode spikes every 10 floors. Ensure your units have high-damage traits like **Extremist** (+100% Damage) before attempting floors 50 and above.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What is the fastest way to get Gems for summoning?",
    answer: "The fastest way is to redeem all active codes (like RELEASE and UPDATE1) and then focus on the main story quests in the most recent world you have unlocked. Raids also provide consistent Gem income."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I get the 'Fortune' trait for my luck team?",
    answer: "You must use Trait Rerolls on your units. These can be obtained from codes, daily missions, or by exchanging Medals earned in Raids."
  },
  {
    question: "Can I get Secret units from the regular banner?",
    answer: "Yes, but the drop rate is extremely low (usually below 0.1%). It is highly recommended to only pull for Secrets when you have your Luck Team equipped and a Luck Potion active."
  },
  {
    question: "Is it better to sell or fuse my extra Legendary units?",
    answer: "In the early game, fuse them to level up your main team quickly. In the late game (World 3+), sell them to acquire Legendary Souls, which are necessary for evolving your top-tier units."
  }
]} />

</Section>
